content:
10. ‘Option + drag’ the ‘Arpeggiator’ object to make a copy. Do this 2 more times so there are 4 ‘Arpeggiator’ objects in total. Name the ‘Arpeggiator’ objects ARP1, ARP2, ARP3, and ARP4.

11. Cable to ‘Monitor’ to the 4 ‘Arpeggiator’ objects. Connect them in order; first ARP1, then ARP2, etc.

12. Now, cable each of the 4 ‘Arpeggiator’ objects to the 4 Ultrabeat Software Instrument tracks.

13. Return to the ‘Main’ window. Create a new track of any kind.

14. ‘Control-click’ on the new track’s track header, and choose ‘Reassign Track > Mixer > Monitor’.

*You are now feeding the MIDI on that track to the ‘Monitor’ object in the MIDI Environment.

15. Select the ‘Monitor’ object track in the track list.

16. Show the ‘Step Input Keyboard’. Go to main menu: ‘Window > Show Step Input Keyboard’.

17. Set the Step Input Keyboard to ‘whole note’, and ‘Chord Mode’.

18. Press ‘Return’ to move the Playhead to the beginning of the project.
context: From document: CREATING EVOLVING DRUM PATTERNS IN ULTRABEAT USING THE MIDI ENVIRONMENT — chunk 2 (chunk 2/3)
